skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Create and refine Codex skills with guidance for structure, triggering, reusable resources, validation, packaging, and iteration.
skill.summary: Build effective Codex skills with clear triggers, lean instructions, resources, validation, and packaging.
skill.author: openai

security_audit.summary: This official skill provides guidance and local helper scripts for creating, validating, and packaging Codex skills. Static analysis found local file creation, file reading, and zip packaging behavior, but no network access, credential access, prompt injection, or command execution patterns.

content.value_statement: Building useful AI skills requires clear triggers, lean instructions, and reliable validation. This skill guides Codex through planning, scaffolding, testing, packaging, and improving reusable skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to design concise skill instructions with clear trigger descriptions.
Guides selection of scripts, references, and assets for reusable skill workflows.
Provides an initialization script that creates a starter skill folder and SKILL.md template.
Provides validation guidance for frontmatter, naming rules, and description quality.
Provides a packaging script that validates a skill and creates a distributable skill archive.
Supports iterative improvement through real usage and targeted testing.
limitations: It does not decide product requirements without user examples or domain context.
Generated starter content still needs review and customization before publishing.
The helper scripts operate on local files and do not publish skills to a marketplace.
Advanced validation depends on the quality of user-provided tests and examples.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated team workflow into a reusable Codex skill with clear instructions and resources.

use_cases.title: Refine an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Improve a skill that triggers poorly, has long instructions, or misses needed validation steps.

use_cases.title: Package Skills for Sharing
use_cases.description: Validate folder structure and create a portable skill archive for review or distribution.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new reusable workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-creator to create a Codex skill for summarizing weekly support tickets. Ask only the key setup questions first.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Skill Resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that needs scripts, references, or assets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-creator to plan a skill for generating compliance reports. Identify which instructions, scripts, and references should be included.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Triggering and Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate update to an existing skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-creator to review this existing skill folder. Improve the description, reduce unnecessary context, and keep resources discoverable.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Package a Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release workflow for a completed skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-creator to validate this skill, fix any structural issues, package it, and explain any remaining release risks.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for handling recurring invoice review tasks.
output: A hyphen-case skill name such as invoice-review.
A concise trigger description for invoice review workflows.
A lean instruction outline with optional references for accounting rules.
A recommendation to add a validation checklist before packaging.

output_examples.input: Review my existing analytics skill and make it easier to trigger.
output: A revised description that states when Codex should use the skill.
Suggestions to move detailed schema notes into reference files.
A validation pass covering frontmatter, naming, and folder structure.

output_examples.input: Prepare this completed skill for distribution.
output: Validation results for the skill folder.
A packaged skill archive when validation passes.
A short release note with remaining manual checks.

best_practices: Start with concrete user examples before writing the skill body.
Keep core instructions concise and move detailed material into referenced files.
Run validation and test helper scripts before packaging a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not add broad background material that Codex already knows.
Do not create extra documentation files that are not needed by the skill.
Do not package placeholder resources without replacing or deleting them.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create Codex skills that combine instructions, reusable resources, validation steps, and packaging guidance.

faq.question: Can it update an existing skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide edits to descriptions, structure, resources, validation, and packaging workflows.

faq.question: Does it generate starter files?
faq.answer: Yes. The included initializer can create a starter skill folder, SKILL.md template, and optional resource directories.

faq.question: Does it publish skills automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can validate and package local skill files, but publishing remains a separate process.

faq.question: Is it only for technical skills?
faq.answer: No. It can support coding, writing, operations, research, and domain workflow skills.

faq.question: Why does it include scripts?
faq.answer: Scripts make repeated setup, validation, and packaging steps more reliable than rewriting commands each time.
